name: DataRobot API
what_it_does: Enterprise AI platform with REST API for automated model training, MLOps deployment, AI governance, monitoring, and lifecycle management across the full ML pipeline.
use_cases: Triggering AutoML experiments programmatically with tabular data for agent-driven ML workflows
Deploying trained models to production endpoints via API without manual steps
Monitoring model drift and data quality in production deployments from automation agents
Managing AI governance workflows (model documentation, bias assessments, approvals)
Comparing champion/challenger models for automated model replacement decisions
not_for: Deep learning research (DataRobot focuses on enterprise-grade tabular/structured data)
Custom neural network architectures requiring PyTorch/TensorFlow control
best_when: An enterprise agent needs production-grade ML automation with governance, explainability, and monitoring built in.
avoid_when: You need open-source flexibility, are a startup, or work primarily with unstructured data like text or images.

auth.notes: API tokens generated from DataRobot account. User-level tokens with role-based access control. Enterprise SSO integration available. Tokens have configurable expiry.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise licensing from $100k-500k+/year; cloud consumption pricing also available
pricing.notes: DataRobot offers both cloud (DataRobot AI Platform) and on-premises deployment. Competitive with H2O Driverless AI and SAS Viya in the enterprise AutoML market.

performance.rate_limits: Per-tier rate limits; documented in customer portal

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is comprehensive. Python SDK handles most async patterns well. Error responses are structured JSON with codes, messages, and resolution hints.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Model training projects are identifiable by name. Deployment endpoints are stable once created. Agents should check for existing resources before creating duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: AutoML training is async — agents must poll job status and may wait hours for completion
Model training costs can be significant at scale — agents should implement training budgets
Feature engineering transformations must be specified; DataRobot auto-discovers but can be controlled
Prediction explanations add significant latency — only request when needed

security_score_components.security_notes: FedRAMP authorized for government use. SOC2 Type II and HIPAA compliant. RBAC with fine-grained permissions. Enterprise SSO (SAML/OIDC). Data residency options in US/EU/APAC.
